factor_findings.message: Soil Ca:B = 2800 exceeds 1000:1 — Ca-induced B deficiency likely, especially at flowering.
factor_findings.recommended_action: Apply foliar B at critical stages (pre-bloom, fruit set)
factor_findings.source_section: Plant & Soil 193:121-148

factor_findings.message: pH (KCl) 4.80 below crop-optimal band (5.0–6.5). Acidic soil restricts Ca / Mg / P availability; root expansion limited.
factor_findings.recommended_action: Lime application (calcitic or dolomitic per Mg status). Size against pH deficit + buffer factor; pre-season for reaction time.
factor_findings.source_section: FERTASA Soil Fertility Handbook §5.1 — Liming and pH management

headline_signals: Soil Ca:B = 2800 exceeds the 1000:1 ceiling — Ca-induced B deficiency is likely at flowering, with downstream impacts on pollen viability, kernel set, and the eventual nut-fill window. Recommend foliar B at pre-bloom + 50%-bloom + 4 wk later, with a soil-Ca audit at the next sampling pass to verify the antagonism is the dominant driver here and not a parallel B deficiency masked by limited soil-test sensitivity.

foliar_events.trigger_reason: Soil Ca:B = 2800 exceeds the 1000:1 ceiling and is the dominant antagonism here. Pre-bloom B foliar is the standard SA citrus intervention; the timing is keyed off the cumulative degree-days since bud-break and not the calendar week. We apply at 50% bloom and again 28 days later to cover pollen viability and the early fruit-set window. This is exactly the kind of long, detailed rationale Opus will produce when given freedom to explain the why. Soil Ca:B = 2800 exceeds the 1000:1 ceiling and is the dominant antagonism here. Pre-bloom B foliar is the standard SA citrus intervention; the timing is keyed off the cumulative degree-days since bud-break and not the calendar week. We apply at 50% bloom and again 28 days later to cover pollen viability and the early fruit-set window. This is exactly the kind of long, detailed rationale Opus will produce when given freedom to explain the why. Soil Ca:B = 2800 exceeds the 1000:1 ceiling and is the dominant antagonism here. Pre-bloom B foliar is the standard SA citrus intervention; the timing is keyed off the cumulative degree-days since bud-break and not the calendar week. We apply at 50% bloom and again 28 days later to cover pollen viability and the early fruit-set window. This is exactly the kind of long, detailed rationale Opus will produce when given freedom to explain the why. Soil Ca:B = 2800 exceeds the 1000:1 ceiling and is the dominant antagonism here. Pre-bloom B foliar is the standard SA citrus intervention; the timing is keyed off the cumulative degree-days since bud-break and not the calendar week. We apply at 50% bloom and again 28 days later to cover pollen viability and the early fruit-set window. This is exactly the kind of long, detailed rationale Opus will produce when given freedom to explain the why.
